figures.caption: Figure 1: Illustration of Composed Visual Retrieval.
Table 3: Performance comparison on CIRR and CIRCO.
Table 2: Performance comparison on the validation set of FashionIQ. The best results are in bold, while the second-best results are underlined.
Figure 5: Pseudo triplets of Type I (Fashion200K). Circled letters (A, B, and C) indicate the generating prompts.

Figure 8: PCA visualization of embedding distributions before and after the ReAlign transformation on (a) LLaVAPretrain and (b) Fashion200K. Yellow triangles and orange squares denote text embeddings before and after ReAlign, respectively, while blue circles show target image embeddings. Stars mark cluster centroids.
Table 4: Performance comparison on Multi-Turn FashionIQ.
Table 11: Performance comparison under different scoring budgets 𝐾 ′ 1/𝐾 ′ 2 on the FashionIQ validation set.
Table 13: Performance comparison under different scoring budgets 𝐾 ′ 1/𝐾 ′ 2 on Multi-Turn FashionIQ.
Table 12: Performance comparison under different scoring budgets 𝐾 ′ 1/𝐾 ′ 2 on CIRR and CIRCO.
Figure 4: Pseudo triplets of Type I (LLaVA-Pretrain). Circled letters (A, B, and C) indicate the generating prompts.

Table 6: Ablation study results. For FashionIQ and MT-FashionIQ, we report the average across three categories. For CIRR, we report the average of R@1/5/10/50 and Rsubset@1/2/3. For WebVid-CoVR, we report the average of R@1/5/10/50. Avg. is the mean of all individual metrics.

Figure 2: Overview of UniCVR. Stage I conducts pre-training to bridge the heterogeneous embedding spaces between the MLLM query encoder and the PEcore gallery encoder. Stage II performs dual-level reranking that exploits MLLM relevance judgments from initial retrieval results, refining the ranking by both the global and local re-scoring.

Figure 9: Case studies on FashionIQ. Each case shows the composed query (left), Stage I ranking with cosine similarities (top-5 results), Stage II w/-LocalOnly ranking with MLLM relevance scores (top-5 results), and the final Stage II ranking with dual-level re-scored values (top-10 results). Target images are highlighted in green boxes. Below Stage II results, rank changes from Stage I→Stage II are annotated via background color: green = improvement, blue = unchanged, brown = demotion.
Figure 10: Case studies on WebVid-CoVR. The layout follows the same format as Figure 9.
Table 7: Detailed ablation study results on the validation set of FashionIQ.
Table 8: Detailed ablation study results on CIRR and CIRCO.
Table 9: Detailed ablation study results on Multi-Turn FashionIQ.
Table 10: Detailed ablation study results on WebVid-CoVR.
